comment: Allow-list for plugins loaded from data/plugins/ or installed via npm. Each entry maps a plugin id to either a public key (verify ed25519 signature against this key) or a content-hash pin (sha256 of the manifest's canonical JSON form). Strict mode in the loader requires every plugin to have an entry here. The two example entries below cover the in-tree demo plugins shipped under data/plugins/; remove them in production deployments.

ai-video.example.hello-stage.note: demo stage plugin shipped with the repo — registers PLUGIN_HELLO after REFINEMENT

ai-video.example.echo-provider.note: demo provider plugin shipped with the repo — registers an echo chat adapter
